title: Mycorrhizal Intelligence
definition: The collective intelligence of interconnected entities that mimics the symbiotic relationship between fungi and plants, enabling efficient information exchange, learning, and adaptability.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Think of a forest where trees communicate through their roots. Mycorrhizal intelligence in CADMIES is like that network of roots—it enables the AI to learn from various sources and adapt intelligently.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Mycorrhizal Intelligence is an artificial intelligence architecture inspired by mycorrhizal networks in nature. It allows for efficient information exchange between interconnected entities, facilitating learning and adaptation through collective intelligence.
difficulty_levels.expert: Inspired by the mutualistic relationship between fungi and plants, mycorrhizal intelligence is a novel approach to AI that prioritizes collaboration and adaptability. It enables distributed knowledge networks where information can be shared and learned from across multiple sources, facilitating more efficient and resilient problem-solving.

extra_fields.insight: An innovative AI design inspired by natural symbiosis, promoting learning and adaptation through collective intelligence.
